steps.title: Use the circumference formula
steps.content: The circumference of a circle is $C=\pi d$, where $d$ is the diameter. Substitute the given circumference $36\pi$.

steps.title: Apply the circumference formula
steps.content: The circumference of a circle is $C=\pi d$, where $d$ is the diameter. Substitute the given $C=36\pi$: $$36\pi=\pi d.$$

steps.title: Solve for the diameter
steps.content: Divide both sides by $\pi$: $$d=36.$$ The answer is 36.
